parsing_res_list.block_content: trations of plasmid DNA ranging from1to 20ng while keeping the primer concentration constant; generally, 5and 10 ng of plasmid DNA give good results.

In summ o PCR-based site-directed mutagenesis used to splice together different regions of a gene in a one-tube PCR. In comparison with methods now available for assembling DNA segments of a gene in tandem, it is simple, convenient, and time-saving. As no nearby restriction sites are required or engineered into the mutagenic primer, a deletion can be introduced anywhere within the sequence of interest. If more than three DNA segments of a gene are to be assembled in tandem, one more round of PCR mutagenesis with the desired mutagenic primer should be performed using the mutant plasmid DNA obtained from the last round of PCR mutagenesis as template. We routinely use this procedure in our laboratory with great success.
This work was partially supported by the Key Project of Developing Agriculture through Science and Technology of Shanghai.

[1] R.M. Horton, H.D. Hunt, S.N. Ho, J.K. Pullen, L.R. Pease,Engineering hybrid genes without the use of restriction enzymes: gene splicing by overlap extension, Gene 77 (1989) 61–68.[2] K.L. Heckman, L.R. Pease, Gene splicing and mutagenesis by PCRdriven overlap extension, Nat. Protocols 2 (2007) 924–932.[3] E.N. Lebedenko, K.R. Birikh, O.V. Plutalov, Yu.A. Berlin, Method of artificial DNA splicing by directed ligation (SDL), Nucleic Acids Res. 19 (1991) 6757–6761.
[4] A.Urban,S.Neukirchen, K.Jaeger, Arapidandefficientmethod for site directed mutagenesis using one-step overlap extension PCR,
Nucleic Acids Res. 25 (1997) 2227–2228.
[5] G. Sarkar, S.S. Sommer, The "megaprimer" method of site-directed mutagenesis, Biotechniques 8 (1990) 404–407.
[6] J. Braman, C. Papworth, A. Greener, Site-directed mutagenesis using double-stranded plasmid DNA templates, Methods Mol. Biol. 57(1996) 31–44.
[7] S. Li, M.F. Wilkinson, Site-directed mutagenesis: a two-step metod using PCR and DpnI, Biotechniques 23 (1997) 588–590.[8] Z. Ma, H. Izumi, M. Kanai, Y. Kabuyama, N.G. Ahn, K. Fukasawa,Mortalincontrols centrosome duplication via modulatingcentrosomal localization of p53, Oncogene 39 (2006) 5377–5390.[9] Z. Ma, M. Kanai, K. Kawamura, K. Kaibuchi, K.Ye, K. Fukasawa,Interaction between ROCK II and nucleophosmin/B23 in the regulation of centrosome duplication, Mol. Cell Biol. 23 (2006)9016–9034.
